1	//! Initialization of standard libraries for Lua.
2	//!
3	//! Opens all standard libraries via `require`-style loading and registers
4	//! them into the global table.
5	//!
6	//! Port of `src/linit.c` (66 lines, 1 function).
7	
8	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
9	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
10	
11	// C: lua_CFunction — fn pointer type for Lua-callable C functions.
12	// Matches types.tsv: lua_CFunction → fn(&mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError>
13	type LuaCFunction = fn(&mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError>;
14	
15	// ── Library-name byte-string constants ────────────────────────────────────
16	//
17	// These replace the C macros from lualib.h and lauxlib.h:
18	//   LUA_GNAME        = "_G"         (lauxlib.h)
19	//   LUA_LOADLIBNAME  = "package"    (lualib.h)
20	//   LUA_COLIBNAME    = "coroutine"  (lualib.h)
21	//   LUA_TABLIBNAME   = "table"      (lualib.h)
22	//   LUA_IOLIBNAME    = "io"         (lualib.h)
23	//   LUA_OSLIBNAME    = "os"         (lualib.h)
24	//   LUA_STRLIBNAME   = "string"     (lualib.h)
25	//   LUA_MATHLIBNAME  = "math"       (lualib.h)
26	//   LUA_UTF8LIBNAME  = "utf8"       (lualib.h)
27	//   LUA_DBLIBNAME    = "debug"      (lualib.h)
28	//
29	// Per PORTING.md §3.1 all Lua string data uses &[u8], not &str.
30	
31	// C: static const luaL_Reg loadedlibs[] = {
32	//   {LUA_GNAME, luaopen_base},
33	//   {LUA_LOADLIBNAME, luaopen_package},
34	//   {LUA_COLIBNAME, luaopen_coroutine},
35	//   {LUA_TABLIBNAME, luaopen_table},
36	//   {LUA_IOLIBNAME, luaopen_io},
37	//   {LUA_OSLIBNAME, luaopen_os},
38	//   {LUA_STRLIBNAME, luaopen_string},
39	//   {LUA_MATHLIBNAME, luaopen_math},
40	//   {LUA_UTF8LIBNAME, luaopen_utf8},
41	//   {LUA_DBLIBNAME, luaopen_debug},
42	//   {NULL, NULL}
43	// };
44	//
45	// PORT NOTE: C sentinel `{NULL, NULL}` dropped — Rust slices carry their
46	//   own length, so no terminator is needed.
47	//
48	// PORT NOTE: Per PORTING.md §7, `luaopen_X` → `open` inside the module
49	//   (e.g. `crate::base::open`, `crate::string_lib::open`).  As of Phase A
50	//   the individual stdlib modules exported inconsistent names:
51	//     base.rs        → `pub fn open`          (canonical; matches here)
52	//     string_lib.rs  → `pub fn luaopen_string` (needs rename in Phase B)
53	//     table_lib.rs   → `pub fn open_table`    (needs rename in Phase B)
54	//     math_lib.rs    → `pub fn luaopen_math`  (needs rename in Phase B)
55	//     io_lib.rs      → `pub fn luaopen_io`    (needs rename in Phase B)
56	//     os_lib.rs      → `pub fn open_os`       (needs rename in Phase B)
57	//     utf8_lib, debug_lib, coro_lib, loadlib  → not yet ported (Phase B)
58	//   Phase B should rename every stdlib opener to `pub fn open` and update
59	//   this table accordingly.
60	static LOADED_LIBS: &[(&[u8], LuaCFunction)] = &[
61	    (b"_G",         crate::base::open),
62	    (b"package",    crate::loadlib::luaopen_package),
63	    (b"coroutine",  crate::coro_lib::open_coroutine),
64	    (b"table",      crate::table_lib::open_table),
65	    (b"io",         crate::io_lib::luaopen_io),
66	    (b"os",         crate::os_lib::open_os),
67	    (b"string",     crate::string_lib::luaopen_string),
68	    (b"math",       crate::math_lib::luaopen_math),
69	    (b"utf8",       crate::utf8_lib::open_utf8),
70	    (b"debug",      crate::debug_lib::open_debug),
71	];
72	
73	// C: LUALIB_API void luaL_openlibs (lua_State *L) {
74	//   const luaL_Reg *lib;
75	//   /* "require" functions from 'loadedlibs' and set results to global table */
76	//   for (lib = loadedlibs; lib->func; lib++) {
77	//     luaL_requiref(L, lib->name, lib->func, 1);
78	//     lua_pop(L, 1);  /* remove lib */
79	//   }
80	// }
81	//
82	// PORT NOTE: `LUALIB_API` → `pub` (PORTING.md §4.1 / macros.tsv).
83	//   `luaL_requiref(L, name, func, 1)` → `state.require_lib(name, func, true)?`
84	//   The final `1` argument means "set global" — the loaded module value is
85	//   assigned to the global table under `name` and the value left on the
86	//   stack is then discarded by `lua_pop(L, 1)`.
87	//   `lua_pop(L, 1)` → `state.pop_n(1)` (macros.tsv).
88	/// Open all standard Lua libraries into `state`, registering each into the
89	/// global table.
90	///
91	/// Corresponds to `luaL_openlibs` in `linit.c`.
92	pub fn open_libs(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
93	    for &(name, func) in LOADED_LIBS {
94	        // C: luaL_requiref(L, lib->name, lib->func, 1);
95	        state.require_lib(name, func, true)?;
96	        // C: lua_pop(L, 1);  /* remove lib */
97	        state.pop_n(1);
98	    }
99	    Ok(())
100	}
101	
102	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
103	// PORT STATUS
104	//   source:        src/linit.c  (66 lines, 1 function)
105	//   target_crate:  lua-stdlib
106	//   confidence:    high
107	//   todos:         1
108	//   port_notes:    3
109	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
110	//   notes:         Trivial file. Cross-crate refs (state.require_lib,
111	//                  state.pop_n, crate::*::open) resolve in Phase B.
112	//                  Phase B must also reconcile inconsistent open-function
113	//                  names in the existing stdlib modules (see PORT NOTEs).
114	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115

989	// ── Module opener ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
990	
991	/// Open the base library: register all base functions into the global table,
992	/// then set `_G` (a self-reference) and `_VERSION`.
993	///
994	/// C: `LUAMOD_API int luaopen_base(lua_State *L)`
995	pub fn open(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
996	    // C: lua_pushglobaltable(L);
997	    state.push_globals()?;
998	    // C: luaL_setfuncs(L, base_funcs, 0);
999	    state.set_funcs(BASE_FUNCS, 0)?;
1000	    // C: lua_pushvalue(L, -1); lua_setfield(L, -2, LUA_GNAME);
1001	    state.push_copy(-1)?;
1002	    state.set_field(-2, LUA_GNAME)?;
1003	    // C: lua_pushliteral(L, LUA_VERSION); lua_setfield(L, -2, "_VERSION");
1004	    state.push_string(LUA_VERSION_STR)?;
1005	    state.set_field(-2, b"_VERSION")?;
1006	    Ok(1)
1007	}
1008

1024	//                  directly (Phase B), (5) pcallk / callk continuations are
1025	//                  stubbed pending coroutine support in Phase E.  The fake
1026	//                  `struct LuaState;` placeholder here avoids duplicate-definition
1027	//                  errors while keeping the file self-contained; Phase B removes it.

1081	// ── Meta-field and misc helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1082	
1083	/// Push the metafield `event` of `obj` onto the stack and return its type.
1084	/// If there is no metafield, nothing is pushed and `LuaType::Nil` is returned.

1086	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_getmetafield(lua_State *L, int obj, const char *event)`
1087	pub fn get_metafield(
1088	    state: &mut LuaState,
1089	    obj: i32,

1020	/// Internal chunk reader that returns a single buffer slice then signals EOF.
1021	///
1022	/// C: `static const char *getS(lua_State *L, void *ud, size_t *size)`
1023	fn make_string_reader(data: Vec<u8>) -> impl FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
1024	    let mut remaining = Some(data);
1025	    move || remaining.take()
1026	}
1027	
1028	/// Load a file as a Lua chunk. Returns `LUA_OK` on success or an error code.
1029	///
1030	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadfilex(lua_State *L, const char *filename, const char *mode)`
1031	// TODO(port): uses std::fs which is banned outside lua-cli; stub for Phase A.
1032	pub fn load_filex(
1033	    state: &mut LuaState,
1034	    filename: Option<&[u8]>,
1035	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1036	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1037	    // TODO(port): open file with std::fs::File, handle BOM, skip shebang line.
1038	    // For Phase A we always fail cleanly.
1039	    let _ = (filename, mode);
1040	    Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
1041	        "luaL_loadfilex not implemented in Phase A (std::fs banned outside lua-cli)"
1042	    )))
1043	}
1044	
1045	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk.
1046	///
1047	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadbufferx(lua_State *L, const char *buff, size_t size, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1048	pub fn load_bufferx(
1049	    state: &mut LuaState,
1050	    buff: &[u8],
1051	    name: &[u8],
1052	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1053	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1054	    // C: LoadS ls; ls.s = buff; ls.size = size;
1055	    // C: return lua_load(L, getS, &ls, name, mode);
1056	    // TODO(phase-b): state.load expects (chunk: &[u8], name, mode) in state_stub; the reader-based loader needs a load_with_reader API match.
1057	    let _reader = make_string_reader(buff.to_vec());
1058	    let ok = state.load(buff, name, mode)?;
1059	    Ok(if ok { 0 } else { 1 })
1060	}
1061	
1062	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk (no mode argument).
1063	///
1064	/// C: `#define luaL_loadbuffer(L,s,sz,n) luaL_loadbufferx(L,s,sz,n,NULL)`
1065	pub fn load_buffer(
1066	    state: &mut LuaState,
1067	    buff: &[u8],
1068	    name: &[u8],
1069	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1070	    load_bufferx(state, buff, name, None)
1071	}
1072

1076	pub fn load_string(state: &mut LuaState, s: &[u8]) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1077	    // C: return luaL_loadbuffer(L, s, strlen(s), s);

1621	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1622	///
1623	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1624	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1625	    state: &mut LuaState,
1626	    z: ZIO,
1627	    name: &[u8],
1628	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1629	) -> LuaStatus {
1630	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1631	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1632	    state.inc_nny();
1633	
1634	    let mut p = SParser {
1635	        z,
1636	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1637	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1638	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1639	        name: name.to_vec(),
1640	    };
1641	
1642	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1643	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1644	
1645	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1646	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1647	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1648	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1649	
1650	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1651	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1652	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1653	
1654	    // C: decnny(L)
1655	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1656	    state.dec_nny();

2802	/// Convert a `lua_lex::TokenValue` into the local `parse::TokenValue` flat shape.
2803	///
2804	/// The parser's local `LexState` predates the lex-side enum and uses a flat
2805	/// (r, i, ts) record; this picks out whichever variant the lexer produced.
2806	fn local_token_value(v: &lua_lex::TokenValue) -> TokenValue {
2807	    match v {
2808	        lua_lex::TokenValue::None => TokenValue::default(),
2809	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Float(r) => TokenValue { r: *r, i: 0, ts: None },
2810	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Int(i) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: *i, ts: None },
2811	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Str(s) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: 0, ts: Some(GcRef(s.clone())) },
2812	    }
2813	}

1	//! `LuaProto` — compiled function prototype. Mirrors C-Lua's `Proto` struct
2	//! but uses Rust idioms (Vec instead of pointer+size pairs).

11	    pub numparams: u8,

13	    pub maxstacksize: u8,

15	    pub k: Vec<LuaValue>,
16	    pub code: Vec<Instruction>,
17	    pub p: Vec<GcRef<LuaProto>>,
18	    pub lineinfo: Vec<i8>,
19	    pub abslineinfo: Vec<AbsLineInfo>,

24	}

28	        LuaProto {
29	            numparams: 0,
30	            is_vararg: false,

34	            code: Vec::new(),

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:1591` —
`f_parser` parses the chunk via `parse_stub` / `undump` (which return the
`GcRef<LuaLClosure>` by value), then calls `init

_upvals` and returns without ever pushing the closure to the stack.

In C-Lua, `luaY_par
ser` itself pushes the closure (`setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L)`). The Rust port shifted the clos
ure to a return value but no caller pushes it. As a result, after `state
.protected_parser` succeeds, `top - 1` still holds wh
atever stale `Nil` was there, so `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` finds Nil at the call

slot and `try_func_tm` raises `"attempt to call a nil value"`.

**Fix:** After par

sing succeeds (both binary and text branches), push the closure onto the stack:
```rust
state.check_stack(1)?;
state.push(Lua
ity `debug_assert!` out of the if/else so both branches share the same finalization,

then push.

**New error:** `[panic] not yet implemented: phase-b: set_ci_savedpc` — exec
ution now reaches the VM interpreter loop with a real closure at top-1; the next
blocker is the `set_ci_savedpc` stub in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs`, which the next
debug iteration will pick up.

content.text:
The fix works — the original error is gone, replaced by a different one. No temporary eprintln to remove (I localized via reading and reasoning, not instrumentation).

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:1591` — `f_parser` parses the chunk via `parse_stub` / `undump` (which return the `GcRef<LuaLClosure>` by value), then calls `init_upvals` and returns without ever pushing the closure to the stack.

In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` itself pushes the closure (`setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L)`). The Rust port shifted the closure to a return value but no caller pushes it. As a result, after `state.protected_parser` succeeds, `top - 1` still holds whatever stale `Nil` was there, so `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` finds Nil at the call slot and `try_func_tm` raises `"attempt to call a nil value"`.

**Fix:** After parsing succeeds (both binary and text branches), push the closure onto the stack:
```rust
state.check_stack(1)?;
state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
```
Hoisted `init_upvals` and the upvals/proto-arity `debug_assert!` out of the if/else so both branches share the same finalization, then push.

**New error:** `[panic] not yet implemented: phase-b: set_ci_savedpc` — execution now reaches the VM interpreter loop with a real closure at top-1; the next blocker is the `set_ci_savedpc` stub in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs`, which the next debug iteration will pick up.

The fix works — the original error is gone, replaced by a different one. No temporary eprintln to remove (I localized via reading and reasoning, not instrumentation).

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:1591` — `f_parser` parses the chunk via `parse_stub` / `undump` (which return the `GcRef<LuaLClosure>` by value), then calls `init_upvals` and returns without ever pushing the closure to the stack.

In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` itself pushes the closure (`setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L)`). The Rust port shifted the closure to a return value but no caller pushes it. As a result, after `state.protected_parser` succeeds, `top - 1` still holds whatever stale `Nil` was there, so `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` finds Nil at the call slot and `try_func_tm` raises `"attempt to call a nil value"`.

**Fix:** After parsing succeeds (both binary and text branches), push the closure onto the stack:
```rust
state.check_stack(1)?;
state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
```
Hoisted `init_upvals` and the upvals/proto-arity `debug_assert!` out of the if/else so both branches share the same finalization, then push.

**New error:** `[panic] not yet implemented: phase-b: set_ci_savedpc` — execution now reaches the VM interpreter loop with a real closure at top-1; the next blocker is the `set_ci_savedpc` stub in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs`, which the next debug iteration will pick up.
